title: The treatments that make patients look older. What to do instead for great patient results.
filename: The treatments that make patients look older. What to do instead for great patient results..en.vtt
excerpt: Did you know that some aesthetic treatments can actually accidentally make patients look older the first time I saw this was at a conference and the injector very proudly asked the audience to guess the age of his patient after he'd done the procedure and they all guessed older n…

segments.text: did you know that some aesthetic treatments can actually accidentally make patients look older the first time I saw this was at a conference and the injector very proudly asked the audience to guess the age of his patient after he'd done the procedure and they all guessed older now the interesting thing about this as awkward as that moment was is I learned something very important sometimes you can do a good treatment with a good result in isolation in the face that has a knock-on effect that does exactly what you don't want to do and in this case it was about treating the cheeks and not the temples and the relative difference in volume between the cheeks and the temples actually made the patient accidentally look older although her cheeks look better so this is the kind of thing we need to be aware of and careful of as injectors there's treatment that is popular right now that I think is making everybody who gets it look older I feel like you know what I'm going to say no I don't oh but yeah okay that's interesting we're aiming for a sweet spot where you

have volume and definition and just the right balance and what you do with buccal fat removal is that you're basically removing fat which if you did it just right will give you beauty but then as people get older they lose that fat whatever fat is remaining and they end up looking more gaunt so over defined is one of the key pitfalls and it happens a lot I think with new injectors because you learn how to treat the cheeks you're very proud to be able to treat the cheeks someone asks you for treat cheeks and you do the cheeks but you've done it in a 48 year old with a BMI of 19 and they just look older they look skeletal yeah even though they needed cheeks they also need a temple and Sub sub zygoma treatment as well so it's about doing the buccal fat in isolation and not really doing anything else well I imagine with the Buckle fat especially that if you're you know if you're 25 with a really big Buckle that pad that makes you look round you probably have a phase between after your surgery between then and 35 that you look great but when you hit 40 you suddenly miss that fat and it's that whole problem we're trying to just keep things in a sweet spot but things are changing so it can look good for two or

three years and then go too far Liam Payne had this done but not even 30 yet so I obviously you can tell that those pictures are taken hardly any time apart that is before his surgery that is after the book of fat powder removal he looks 10 years older after the book of fat pad but he didn't need to look like he doesn't why does he want to look 10 years older what was his goal here do you think well firstly it's different for men because I can basically as he looks in that left picture he looks like he basically looks quite babyish I think no he looks that looks like a little boy who's done well he's a pop you know he looks like a boy band kind of type who might appeal to 12 year old girls but isn't going to appeal to a full-blooded woman you know yeah I understood but I think he's more attractive on the left than he is on the right because the right just ended it ends up looks a bit odd doesn't it yeah I agree he's just tilted into unhealthy so it doesn't mean there's a lot of over definition goes with you know are

you basically starving or are you know getting much older so there's what I see the level of definition he's got there is so much higher particularly with the volume loss that he looks shockingly older compared with his before a lot of Aging is masculizing and so volume loss increased definition uh lower face proportionately getting bigger upper face getting smaller all those things happen to everyone but in a man it makes you more masculine right and a woman obviously don't want to look more masculine it's just associated with aging yeah so there's lots of women getting this as well I think it's aging them massively so Leah Michelle has had it she's gone from looking quite baby-faced to looking she doesn't look bad but she definitely looks older so what is it they're missing so if they're going for definition they want more definition what is it they're missing that they could have had instead of this well I mean it depends on what they're actually pursuing there's there is obviously a look

obviously a look it's kind of like an adolescent look like so it's kind it's almost an adolescent boy look if I'm honestly it's there's a very skinny phase that you go through which um I've heard this said that this was influenced by essentially by fashion designers who tend to be gay men who like that look who kind of coaxed this idea of slim um basically very slim women which isn't I don't think it's a typical Man's first preference but it becomes the fashion so it's definitely become the fashion and so there's a fashionable element which is it's always it's probably the most confusing thing in Aesthetics is what is objective Beauty and what is fashion and there's always like a core that I think is objective but then there's always an overlap um so what I think you're detecting here if lots of people are doing this is a trend a fashion that within certain circles is valued but isn't probably valued in the mainstream so obviously there is a beauty involved in seeing cheekbones and jaw lines I think it's

tilting Beyond and a lot of fashion is about edginess you know if you think about what they do on stage with fashion it's always something Slightly bizarre that makes you stop and think and then when it basically reaches a High Street it's very filtered down and much more gentle and it's a little bit like that with these sort of celebrities which is they are they're wanting to stop you scrolling and the harder you the more average you look the less likely that's going to happen so there's a pursuit of edginess which to my eye it's gone too far and they've actually downgraded themselves from if they just added another just soften that shadow ever so slightly more let's talk about the other treatments that can Aid you so I think if you're in the UK this was about two years ago now everyone will have seen this influencer so if you look at what she looks like here I think she was 20 at the time I don't think that looks like a 20 year old woman but if you look at this picture of her now she's had all of that dissolved she does now look like a 20 21 year old woman so

she's had cheeks she's obviously had jaw she's got overfilled lips why do you think that has made her look far older than she looks realistically yeah she's quite interesting because her natural face is she's relatively petite proportions so she's not a big feature person so red you know gentle Contours between cheek and jawline and her lips um it all fits as a unit whereas what in fact I actually went viral and made a lot of people very cross because I wrote a Blog on that when it first happened and I said um we can't treat patients like Mr like their Mr Potato Head look I'm Picasso as in you take body parts and stick them on but everyone interpreted that as I was calling her a potato head and I really wasn't but it did I think when you know I have learned this over time that when you use a word it doesn't like the context kind of Fades and uh but I think what happened with her is this Con concept of like oh I have my cheeks done I'll have my

jawline done I'll have my lips done and none of it taking into context the overall picture so yeah you had all those things done and if he looks at a chin or a jawline from one side by itself it can look in fact if you watched this original video it's quite interesting because in different angles she looks normal and then there's this particular screen grab which just highlights that it's wrong um and that I think this happens to a lot of well-intentioned injectors which is that you learn a procedure you pick an area you treat it looks good in certain ways and then you know you suddenly see them on social media and you can see it's overdone and this is because it doesn't fit in with her underlying proportions and it's high it creates a sense of volume loss like I said with the lady that I first when I first noticed this her Temple looks empty here and if you look at the other picture it doesn't and that's because they've just bulked out her cheeks so much and her jawline so you have the sense of volume loss underneath the cheek volume less above the cheek and that's created by just too much volume in the other places why do you think these overfilled lips actually make her

look older or do you think it's just a combination of all of it together there's just aged him I think you can get a type of Aging where the amount the oral aperture your mouth actually gets bigger so you if you picture a very slim athletic 80 year old you know or 55 year old um if they're very slim their mouth sometimes looks proportionally wider so they have a shrinking of cheeks and jawline and their mouth the oral aperture the decrease in resistance if they're big smile is or have big teeth effectively the mouth can be wider than it was when they were younger so it might be a little bit of that um I don't think it's the main thing but it's it is that's a proportionately very wide mouth for her and it that might that maybe that's the only reason I can think of is that the width sometimes looks more as you get older another treatment I think that ages you is if you have botox too young so I remember I lived in Essex about 10 years ago now 12 years ago and The Only Way is

Essex came out around that time and the women on that show were my age at the time about 20. um and when they the name their name would pop up and it said their age I'd be like what no way is that woman my age um but then I was looking at them and they all had Frozen faces do you think there's an element of Botox being done too early that makes the ages you if you think about a youthful face um it's naturally damped by fat so that you have a little bit of movement but also the eyebrow positions and your the way your cheek moves when you smile there's a natural like kind of Botox effect but there's a dampening of muscles that means you don't require as much when you start to freeze everything um you firstly there's The Proven effect that muscle atrophy creates a volume loss effect so you actually lose the volume of the muscle so that can make you look a bit older and easy to cure you just leave a space between treatments it's not like a

permanent thing um and then I suspect the other thing that might be happening is that our brains are so good at picking up patterns that we actually associate there are enough people on TV you've had Botox that they're and they're older that when you start to see younger people doing it your brain just thinks they're older yeah that's because you also think why would they would then if they're 19 20 years old a lot of people do it as a preventative and you know in that kind of circles it's also a status symbol to have had treatment um you don't want to be left behind by your girlfriends who are all doing it so you people do it in clusters and it's not really about the individual patients it's about the tribe doing all the stuff that everyone in that tribe does is there anything else that people do that you think ages them unnecessarily I think the overall theme is just one of if you if you're constantly thinking about treating a long bony Contours chins jaw lines cheeks and never about in between so underneath the cheekbone in the temple for example in the forehead you

naturally create this over defined you know slightly cachexic look so I hope that's helped you understand some of the pitfalls of treatment and how we're constantly maintaining a delicate balance between definition and volume if you understand that you'll help your patients and if you're patient watching this you'll know what not to ask for and you'll understand by the way your clinician is speaking whether they they've taken this into account so I hope that helps don't forget if this has helped you to like And subscribe to ring the bell notification so you'll get more content just like this
